title: काली गायत्री
devanagariText:
॥ श्री काली गायत्री मन्त्र ॥

ॐ महाकाल्यै विद्महे
स्मशानवासिन्यै धीमहि।
तन्नो काली प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ विस्तारित काली गायत्री ॥
ॐ काली महाकाली कालिके कालरूपिणी।
कालरात्रि महारात्रि मोहरात्रि नमोऽस्तु ते॥ १॥

मुण्डमालाविभूषिता खड्गहस्ता दिगम्बरा।
श्मशानवासिनी देवी करालवदना शुभा॥ २॥

रक्तबीजवधे देवि महिषासुरमर्दिनि।
चामुण्डे मुण्डमथिनि प्रसीद मम सर्वदा॥ ३॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
कालीगायत्रिमन्त्रं यः जपेद्भक्तिसमन्वितः।
सर्वभयविनिर्मुक्तो महाकालीकृपां लभेत्॥

॥ इति श्री काली गायत्री मन्त्रः सम्पूर्णः ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Kali Gayatri Mantra ||

Om Mahakalyai Vidmahe
Shmashana-vasinyai Dhimahi.
Tanno Kali Prachodayat.

|| Vistarit Kali Gayatri ||
Om Kali Mahakali Kalike Kala-rupini.
Kalaratri Maharatri Moharatri Namostu Te. || 1 ||

Munda-mala-vibhushita Khadga-hasta Digambara.
Shmashana-vasini Devi Karala-vadana Shubha. || 2 ||

Raktabija-vadhe Devi Mahishasura-mardini.
Chamunde Munda-mathini Prasida Mama Sarvada. || 3 ||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Kali-gayatri-mantram Yah Japed-bhakti-samanvitah.
Sarva-bhaya-vinirmukto Mahakali-kripam Labhet.

|| Iti Shri Kali Gayatri Mantrah Sampurnah ||
hindiMeaning: काली गायत्री मन्त्र में माँ काली का ध्यान है — 'हम महाकाली को जानते हैं, श्मशानवासिनी का ध्यान करते हैं, वे काली हमें प्रेरित करें।' विस्तारित स्तुति में उन्हें कालरात्रि, महारात्रि, मुण्डमालाविभूषिता, रक्तबीजवधकर्त्री, महिषासुरमर्दिनी और चामुण्डा के रूप में स्तुति की गई है। भक्तिपूर्वक जप करने से सर्व भय दूर होते हैं।
benefits: Invokes the fierce protective energy of Goddess Kali
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears and grants fearlessness
Traditionally recited for removal of enemies and negative forces
Powerful for tantric sadhana and spiritual awakening
Invokes mahakali's grace when chanted with devotion
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Kali, Kali Gayatri (काली गायत्री) stands as a powerful Vedic meditation mantra. This sacred composition is sourced from Kalika Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to remove all fears and grant fearlessness as well as destroy enemies and negative forces. Additionally, it is known to invoke the fierce protective energy of Goddess Kali. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Kali Puja and Navratri. With 3 verses and a reading time of about 4 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Kali Gayatri in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Kali Gayatri Mantra in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Powerful Gayatri mantra for Goddess Kali for protection and fearlessness.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Kali Gayatri mantra and what does it mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kali Gayatri (श्री काली गायत्री) is a sacred deity-Gayatri mantra dedicated to Goddess Mahakali, preserved in the Kalika Purana and the broader Shakta tantric tradition. The core mantra is Om Mahakalyai Vidmahe, Shmashana-vasinyai Dhimahi, Tanno Kali Prachodayat — meaning: Om — we know Mahakali, we meditate upon her who dwells in the cremation ground, may that Kali inspire our intellect. Like all deity-Gayatris, it is patterned on the original Savitri Gayatri (Rigveda 3.62.10) but invokes Kali as the supreme power of dissolution, transformation and liberation. The vistarit (extended) version invokes her as Kalaratri, Maharatri, Moharatri, Mundamala-vibhushita and Mahishasura-mardini. (Source: Kalika Purana; Shakta tantric tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Goddess Kali and what is her form?
seoFaqs.answer: Mahakali is the supreme fierce form of the Divine Mother, the first of the ten Mahavidyas, and the consort of Mahakala (Shiva in his time-aspect). Her iconic form is awe-inspiring: digambara (sky-clad/naked), karala-vadana (terrible-faced), mundamala-vibhushita (garlanded with severed heads — fifty in number, representing the fifty letters of the Sanskrit alphabet, that is, all manifest reality), khadga-hasta (sword in hand to cut through ignorance), shmashana-vasini (dweller in the cremation ground — that is, the place where ego-attachment is finally surrendered). She is the slayer of Raktabija and Mahishasura, the dissolver of time itself. Despite her terrifying form, she is the supreme Mother of fierce love. (Sources: Devi Mahatmya; Kalika Purana; Mahanirvana Tantra.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Shmashana-vasini and why is Kali called the dweller of the cremation ground?
seoFaqs.answer: Shmashana-vasini — the dweller of the cremation ground — is one of Kali's most sacred and misunderstood epithets. The shmashana is not merely a place of horror but a profound spiritual symbol: it is where the ego (the false 'I am the body') is finally and absolutely surrendered. Kali dwells there because she is the supreme Mother who receives the soul precisely at the moment of complete surrender — when nothing material is left to cling to. Tantric sadhakas have for millennia performed shmashana-sadhana there to face death directly and break attachment. Theologically, the cremation ground is the heart of the renunciate, where every false identification has been burned away. (Sources: Mahanirvana Tantra; Karpuradi Stotra; classical Shakta theology.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Kali Gayatri be recited for best results?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kali Gayatri is most powerful on Tuesday (Mangalvar — sacred to fierce protective deities), Saturday (Shanivar — sacred to Kali for protection from grahas and negative energies), and on every Amavasya (new moon — Kali's special tithi, especially Krishna-paksha Chaturdashi-Amavasya). Most powerful occasions include Kali Puja (Karthik Amavasya — coinciding with Diwali night in Bengal), Mahanisha Puja, the eighth night (Kalaratri night) of Sharad Navaratri, and Krishna Janmashtami's Kalaratri eve. Traditionally chanted 108 times on a rudraksha or rudraksha-and-coral mala in the late evening or after sunset, facing south or south-east, with a black or red asana, often in a quiet place near a tulsi or peepal. (Sources: Kalika Purana paddhati; Drikpanchang Kali Puja calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Kali Gayatri grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the extended Kali Gayatri promises sarva-bhaya-vinirmukto Mahakali-kripam labhet — complete freedom from all fears and the attainment of the grace of Mahakali. Specific traditional benefits include: (1) absolute fearlessness (abhaya), especially in the face of death, illness and enemies; (2) protection from black magic (abhichara), evil spirits and negative energies; (3) destruction of inner enemies — kama, krodha, lobha, moha, mada, matsarya; (4) success in tantric and yogic sadhana; (5) the breaking of deep karmic blocks; (6) the supreme gift of moksha through complete ego-dissolution; (7) for tantric practitioners, the awakening of Kundalini Shakti. It is regarded as one of the most powerful protective mantras. (Source: Kali Gayatri phalashruti; Kalika Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: Should beginners chant the Kali Gayatri without a guru?
seoFaqs.answer: The classical position of the Shakta tantric tradition is that fierce-deity mantras like the Kali Gayatri are most safely and powerfully chanted with proper diksha (initiation) from a qualified guru, who provides the right anushthana (procedural framework) and acts as a protective container for the immense shakti the mantra invokes. That said, the simpler pancha-akshari Vedic-Gayatri form (Om Mahakalyai Vidmahe...) is widely recited by sincere bhaktas without formal initiation as a daily prayer for protection — Kali responds first and foremost to bhakti, not to credentials. Beginners are advised to start with bhakti-bhava, simple recitation, vegetarian diet on the day of chanting, and sincere surrender — and seek a guru when the heart is drawn deeper. (Sources: Mahanirvana Tantra; modern Shakta acharya guidance.)
